---
title: "genus"
generated: true
description: "Genus $g = (2 -  chi) / 2$ of a closed orientable surface."
---

[‹ back to `nitrix.geometry.topology`](../nitrix-geometry-topology.qmd)

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:genus -->
```python
genus(mesh: Mesh) -> int
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:genus -->
Genus $g = (2 - \chi) / 2$ of a closed orientable surface.

Derives the genus (the number of handles) from the Euler characteristic
$\chi$ returned by [`euler_characteristic`](../nitrix-geometry-topology/euler_characteristic.qmd).  A topological
sphere has $g = 0$, a torus $g = 1$, and so on.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:genus -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|------------|--------|--------|----------|--------------------------------------------------------------|
| `mesh` | `Mesh` | — | — | Triangle mesh, assumed **closed, connected, and orientable** (the genus formula does not apply to a surface with boundary or to a non-manifold mesh). |

**Returns** `int`. The integer genus $g$. Host-side; not differentiable.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->
